```typescript
if (route.view === 'search') {

    const spinnerIcon = document.createElement('i');
    spinnerIcon.className = 'bi bi-arrow-repeat spinning';
    spinnerWrap.appendChild(spinnerIcon);
    resultsEl.appendChild(spinnerWrap);

```

content.text:
## Task: Fix [medium] UI/UX: Search loading spinner is visual-only; screen readers get no 'loading' announcement
## Step: execute

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: UI/UX
Severity: medium
File: src/app/render-orchestrator.ts:79-86
Title: Search loading spinner is visual-only; screen readers get no 'loading' announcement

Finding:
When a search is in flight, sighted users see a centered spinning `bi-arrow-repeat`, but the spinner container is a plain `<div>` with no `role='status'` and no `aria-live` region, so assistive-tech users receive no feedback that work is happening (and the previous results simply vanish with no explanation). WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) calls for status messages to be announced programmatically. Mark the spinner wrapper as a polite live region so its presence is announced when it appears.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/app/render-orchestrator.ts, where the spinner is constructed (the `if (route.view === 'search')` block that builds `spinnerWrap` and `spinnerIcon`), add `spinnerWrap.setAttribute('role', 'status');` and `spinnerWrap.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Loading search results');` after creating `spinnerWrap`. Keep the spinner icon itself hidden from AT per the decorative-icons finding (it already has no text content). Do not change the visual styling.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The existing test suite for the search loading spinner covers the visual presence and lifecycle of the spinner but does not verify the accessibility attributes required by the fix (role='status' and aria-label='Loading search results' on the spinner wrapper). The current tests are well-structured and follow project patterns, but they lack coverage for the new WCAG 4.1.3 status message requirements. The fix instructions specify adding these attributes to the spinner wrapper, yet no test asserts their presence. Additionally, the existing test for aria-hidden='true' on the icon (lines 610-630) is a separate accessibility concern and does not cover the requested live region.
Attempt 2: The existing test suite for the search loading spinner does not verify the accessibility attributes required by the fix. The tests check for the presence of the spinner and icon but do not assert that the spinner wrapper has role='status' and aria-label='Loading search results'. Since the fix specifically adds these attributes to satisfy WCAG 4.1.3, test coverage is missing. The test file should include assertions for these attributes to ensure the accessibility fix is properly validated.
Attempt 3: The production code in src/app/render-orchestrator.ts is correct and complete — the spinner wrapper has role='status', aria-label='Loading search results', and the icon has aria-hidden='true', all with thorough documentation. However, the review feedback history (Attempts 1 and 2) specifically and repeatedly asked for test assertions verifying these accessibility attributes, and those assertions are still absent from src/app/render-orchestration.test.ts. The existing spinner test (lines 546-572) checks for the .search-spinner element and the .bi-arrow-repeat.spinning icon but does NOT assert role='status', aria-label='Loading search results', or aria-hidden='true' on the icon. This is the third review pass and the same gap persists. The natural place to add these is within the existing 'shows a spinner' test (or a dedicated accessibility test), asserting spinner.getAttribute('role') === 'status', spinner.getAttribute('aria-label') === 'Loading search results', and spinningIcon.getAttribute('aria-hidden') === 'true'.

details.diff:
...
 556     expect(spinner, 'a .search-spinner element must exist during an in-flight search').toBeTruthy();
 557     const spinningIcon = spinner!.querySelector('.bi-arrow-repeat.spinning');
 558     expect(spinningIcon, 'the spinner must contain a .bi-arrow-repeat.spinning icon').toBeTruthy();
 559 
+560     // Accessibility (WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages): the spinner wrapper must be a
+561     // polite live region so its appearance is announced to assistive tech — a
+562     // plain <div> is silent and the previous results vanish with no
+563     // explanation. role="status" maps to an implicit aria-live="polite" region,
+564     // and because the wrapper has no text of its own its accessible name must
+565     // come from aria-label. The icon itself is decorative (WCAG 1.3.1) and so
+566     // must be hidden from AT via aria-hidden so the announced live region is
+567     // the only thing screen readers perceive.
+568     expect(spinner!.getAttribute('role'), 'spinner wrapper must be role="status"').toBe('status');
+569     expect(
+570       spinner!.getAttribute('aria-label'),
+571       'spinner wrapper must announce its purpose via aria-label',
+572     ).toBe('Loading search results');
+573     expect(
+574       (spinningIcon as HTMLElement).getAttribute('aria-hidden'),
+575       'decorative spinner icon must be aria-hidden="true"',
+576     ).toBe('true');
+577 
 560     // Release the held fetch — results should render and the spinner should be gone
 561     release();
 562     await flush();
 563 
     ...
